q: Which animal has the strongest bite in the world?
exp: The saltwater crocodile's bite exceeds 3,700 pounds per square inch.
An octopus's blood uses copper (hemocyanin) instead of iron.

q: Which is the only mammal that lays eggs?
exp: The platypus and echidna are monotremes — egg-laying mammals.

q: Which animal can regenerate lost limbs?
exp: Some starfish regrow a whole body from a single arm.
It has been recorded at around 11,000 metres altitude.

q: Which animal has the largest heart of any animal?
exp: A blue whale's heart can weigh about 180 kg.

q: Which animal shares the most DNA with humans?
exp: We share about 98.8% of our DNA with chimpanzees.

q: Which insect can lift many times its own body weight?
exp: Some ants lift 50 times their own weight.

q: Which is among the most poisonous animals (by toxin)?
exp: One frog holds enough toxin to kill several humans.

q: Which animal gets almost all its water from food, rarely drinking?
exp: The kangaroo rat extracts water from the seeds it eats.

q: What is the largest invertebrate in the world?
exp: The giant squid can reach 13 m and has the largest eyes in the animal kingdom.

q: Which creature is biologically 'immortal', able to revert to an earlier life stage?
exp: This jellyfish can revert to its polyp stage, restarting its life cycle.
